title: Dryopteris triangula
intro: Dryopteris triangula, also known as the Triangular Wood Fern, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris triangula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Dryopteris triangula is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and topical creams that are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
